concerns.text: Task T022 implements 'Hotelling's T-squared test' but the Spec (FR-005) and Constitution (Principle VII) mandate a 'paired-sample t-test' (Bonferroni corrected) for individual dimensions. The task silently relaxes the statistical method to a multivariate test, violating the constitutional requirement for specific statistical rigor defined in the project's governing document.

Task T014 ('Implement Plasticity and Stability metric calculation logic') fails to explicitly mandate the 'held-out buffer of older tasks' required by Spec FR-003. Without explicit instruction to implement the history buffer retrieval, the task risks defaulting to checking only the immediate predecessor, which is a semantic weakening of the stability metric definition.

Spec FR-004 mandates execution on 'Omniglot and Mini-ImageNet'. Tasks T020/T021 only describe generating results for Omniglot. There is no task to implement the Mini-ImageNet pipeline or a mechanism to handle the 'verified source' constraint failure, creating a silent drop of a functional requirement.

Success Criteria SC-003 and SC-004 require measuring computation time and memory footprint against specific limits (6h, 7GB). Tasks T032/T033 are vague 'optimization' and 'cleanup' tasks. They lack a specific deliverable to implement automated logging and verification of these metrics, meaning the acceptance criteria for SC-003/SC-004 are not preserved in the task list.

The Spec's edge case for 'covariance matrix singularity' (when baseline accuracy ~0) is split ambiguously between T022 (math) and T024 (reporting). T022 mentions a 'fallback strategy' but does not explicitly mandate the mathematical regularization (e.g., adding epsilon to diagonal) required to prevent the test from crashing, risking a failure to meet the 'no crash' acceptance criterion.
